failure_modes.text: People can fail to understand a concept not because information is missing but because they still lack a usable mental bucket for it.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when the audience lacks a schema.
failure_modes.mitigation: Identify what schema the audience already has and connect the new concept to it explicitly; if no existing schema fits, build one with a simple concrete example first.

failure_modes.text: A vivid analogy can mislead when it transfers familiarity without preserving the mechanism that actually matters.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best when mechanism transfers along with familiarity.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate the analogy by lining up assumptions: check whether the causal mechanism in the source domain actually maps to the target domain, not just the surface pattern.

failure_modes.text: Layered metaphors can increase interpretation burden instead of reducing it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use one analogy per concept and provide counterexamples to mark where the metaphor stops applying; do not stack multiple metaphors for the same idea.

premortem_questions.text: What existing schema does the audience already have that could carry this new idea?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when the audience lacks a schema.

premortem_questions.text: Which important structural boundary would this comparison distort if we pushed it too far?
Is this comparison reducing obscurity, or just replacing one kind of confusion with another?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Analogies are the antidote to abstraction and buzzwords.

heuristics.text: Use a comparison that carries the structural logic of the idea, not just a catchy image.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when mechanism transfers along with familiarity.

heuristics.text: Choose adjacent experience the audience already understands, then bridge into the new concept.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when the audience lacks a schema.

heuristics.text: Use analogy to cut through abstraction and jargon when the idea is sound but still not sticking.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when abstraction is blocking understanding.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunk set close to schema transfer, structural fidelity, and anti-jargon use.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor communication-program sprawl because the raw source already supports compact explanation chunks.
open_questions: The source could support broader teaching chunks, but the Wave 2 surface is cleaner when it stays on transfer quality.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on scaffolding and explainability-oriented models.
antagonists: The source is strongest against abstraction drift and cognitive overload from bad comparisons.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tensions against abstraction and cognitive-load-theory.
